title: Fisher Hill Spring Cleanup Walk
shortSummary: Help clear litter, open walking paths, and prepare planting beds along the Fisher Hill Greenway.
fullDescription:
The Fisher Hill Spring Cleanup Walk brings together residents and volunteers to help prepare neighborhood public spaces for the warmer months.

Participants may assist with litter removal, light landscaping, path clearing, and planting preparation along portions of the Fisher Hill Greenway.

Cleanup supplies and gloves will be available at the meeting area.

title: Highlands Rain Garden Workshop
shortSummary: Learn simple techniques for reducing stormwater runoff using native plants and small residential rain gardens.
fullDescription:
This workshop introduces residents to small-scale rain garden techniques designed for neighborhood homes and shared spaces.

Volunteers and environmental educators will discuss native planting, runoff management, and practical ways residents can reduce localized flooding and erosion.

Printed materials will be available following the presentation.

title: White Plains Emergency Preparedness Night
shortSummary: City staff and volunteers will discuss emergency alerts, cooling centers, severe weather response, and household preparedness.
fullDescription:
Residents are invited to attend an informational evening focused on emergency preparedness and public safety resources available throughout White Plains.

Topics include severe weather notifications, cooling center locations, emergency communications, and household preparedness recommendations.

Representatives from the City and neighborhood organizations will answer questions following the presentation.

title: Gedney Farms Historic Homes Walking Tour
shortSummary: A guided walking tour highlighting several historic homes and neighborhood landmarks within the Gedney Farms area.
fullDescription:
This walking tour explores several historic residences and neighborhood landmarks associated with the development of the Gedney Farms district.

Volunteer guides will provide historical background on architecture, preservation efforts, and neighborhood planning history.

Participants are encouraged to wear comfortable walking shoes.

title: North Street Community Picnic & Music Evening
locationAddress: Lake Street & Turnure Park Entrance, White Plains, NY 10601
shortSummary: Bring blankets and picnic dinners for live acoustic music and family activities in Turnure Park.
fullDescription:
Residents and families are invited to gather for a relaxed community evening featuring acoustic music performances and outdoor picnic seating.

The event is intended as a casual neighborhood gathering and will include open lawn space for children and families.

Attendees are encouraged to bring blankets or folding chairs.

title: Safe Streets Bicycle Visibility Clinic
shortSummary: Volunteers will provide free bicycle light installation, helmet fitting guidance, and visibility tips for riders.
fullDescription:
The Safe Streets Bicycle Visibility Clinic offers residents practical bicycle safety guidance ahead of the summer season.

Volunteers will assist with helmet fitting, visibility recommendations, and free front/rear bicycle light installation while supplies last.

Families and riders of all ages are welcome.

title: Battle Hill Spring Cleanup Walk
shortSummary: Battle Hill Neighborhood Association is hosting a volunteer Spring Cleanup Walk at Battle Hill Park on Sunday, June 14, 2026 at 9:00 AM — gloves and bags provided. All residents and families welcome.
fullDescription: The Battle Hill neighborhood association is organizing a volunteer cleanup morning at Battle Hill Park in White Plains for residents and families. Gloves and bags provided. Come help tidy up the park ahead of summer.

title: Charity soccer tournament at White Plains High School
shortSummary: Charity all-day soccer tournament hosted by Kick for a Kure at White Plains High School on June 13, 2026 (round robin begins at 10 AM), raising money for cancer research — $100 donation per team to enter.
fullDescription: All day soccer tournament to raise money for cancer research through the nonprofit "Kick for a Kure." $100 donation per team for entry. Round robin will begin at 10am, and continue all day until a champion is crowned.
